content:
2 min read
1 hour ago

--

The “Lovable inside your own product” pattern, using an open-source Next.js starter and a single VCaaS key.
Your users keep asking for the same thing: "can I just describe what I want and have your product build it?" Building a Lovable-style AI app builder from scratch is a multi-quarter project: sandboxes, a code-gen agent, hosting, databases, deploy pipelines, GitHub sync. Most teams never ship it.
There is a shortcut I have been recommending: an open-source Next.js app builder you can embed, backed by a single API. (Disclosure: I work at Totalum, which makes it. Facts only below.)
The repo is ai-app-builder-open (MIT-licensed). On its own it is a working "prompt to full-stack app" builder. But the interesting part for software companies is the architecture: all the hard infrastructure, hosting, sandboxes, managed databases, the AI build agent, custom domains, GitHub sync, multitenancy, sits behind one Totalum VCaaS API key (Vibe-Coding-as-a-Service). You own the frontend; the platform handles the backend.
That means two ways to use it. First, run it as-is: your own free, self-hosted v0, Lovable or Bolt alternative. Second, embed it: take the components (or the API) into your existing SaaS and offer "build a full-stack app from a prompt" as a native feature, under your brand, on your domain, with your users isolated by multitenancy.
Why one key matters: normally an app builder needs a hosting provider, a database, a sandbox runtime, an AI provider, a domains/DNS layer and CI. That is six vendors, six bills, six failure points. Here it is one key, kept server-side only.
Getting started as a builder is quick: clone the repo and run npm install, create an account at totalum.app and choose "Use the Totalum API" to copy the key, put TOTALUM_VCAAS_API_KEY in .env and run npm run dev. The first 50 AI credits are free.
If you are a SaaS or an agency, the multitenant plus white-label story is the reason to look: you are not adding a feature, you are adding a platform, in an afternoon instead of a year.
Repo: https://github.com/totalumlabs/ai-app-builder-open
Docs: totalum.app/docs
Honest note: it is new and moving fast, and the AI engine is hosted (that is what the key is for), so this is for teams happy to build on an API rather than a local model. If that is you, it is the fastest path I know from "our users want an app builder" to actually having one.
